figures.caption: Figure 1: Cross-boundary AUC across all four experiments. CAL provides large gains on gene perturbation data where latent associative signal exists (Replogle, DepMap). Drug experiments reveal failure modes: no latent signal (fingerprints) and degree confounding (L1000, where shuffled > real).

Figure 3: Cross-boundary improvement by degree quintile (high confidence ≥900, cross-boundary pairs with |cosine| < 0.2). Low-degree genes (Q1) benefit approximately twice as much as highdegree genes (Q5). The trend is approximately monotonic; Q4 and Q5 show near-equal improvement (+0.124 vs +0.127), suggesting the degree effect saturates for high-degree genes. Table 8 shows Q1 and Q5 from the same high-confidence cross-boundary data; the appendix (Table 17) uses mediumconfidence all-pairs.

Figure 2: Confidence threshold sweep showing quality-over-quantity. (A) Overall AUC and (B) cross-boundary AUC both improve with tighter thresholds despite fewer training pairs. Experimental-only filtering (hatched) matches the highest combined-score threshold.
Table 7: Operating envelope: when does CAL work?

Figure 5: Cross-boundary sensitivity analysis. As the threshold tightens (left to right), cosine AUC declines toward chance while CAL AUC remains stable or slightly increases. Shaded region shows the growing gap.
Table 10: Domain comparison: text vs biology.
Table 17: Full degree quintile analysis (Replogle, medium confidence)—all pairs.
Table 13: CAL AUC at varying cross-boundary thresholds (high confidence ≥900). See also Figure 5.

Figure 4: (A) Association score distributions by cosine similarity bin. In the cross-boundary regime (yellow shading), STRING-positive pairs (blue) show clear separation from non-associated pairs (grey). (B) Three illustrative gene pairs showing CAL lift: each arrow indicates the gain from cosine (grey dot on diagonal) to the actual association score (blue dot).
Table 3: Combined-score vs experimental-channel filtering. See also Figure 2.
Table 2: Confidence threshold sweep (Replogle K562 + STRING). See also Figure 1.
Table 11: Overall AUC as a function of λ (Replogle).
